title: limonium dodartii subsp. lusitanicum
intro: Limonium dodartii subsp. lusitanicum, also known as Sea Lavender, is a plant species that belongs to the Plumbaginaceae family and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is known for its health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. The herbal preparations of Sea Lavender include essential oils, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
